title: The Brew Shop - Weekly Email Update
contentHtml:

The Brew Shop Weekly Update

Hey Brew Shop Fam!
Here's an update of what's happening this week - from new beer drops, wine reviews, our current tap list, to upcoming tastings, and more. 

SHOP THIS WEEK’S NEWSLETTER HERE
SHOP OUR ENTIRE STOCK OF BEER, WINE & HOMEBREW HERE
 
6 Roses of Spring!

We're excited to bring back The Brew Shop 6 Roses of Spring! Nothing hails the beginning of warm weather quite like Rose and we're here to help you ring it in with 6 fresh Roses. Available in limited quantities by pre-order, make sure you grab yours before they sell out!

Our 6 Roses of Spring will be hand-selected by your favorite winos (Beth, Julie & Aidan) and filled with never-before-seen-in-the-shop Rose. What's included is a surprise, but each pack will contain ONE sparkler and FIVE still Roses.

6 Roses of Spring packs will be available for pick-up beginning Thursday, May 7th - just in time for Mother's Day, if that special Mom in your life adores Rose!Wine Club Member? Remember to put in your special code at check out for 10% off!

PRE-ORDER 6 ROSES OF SPRING HERE
 

Plan ahead Arlington! We’ll be closed this Sunday for a staff outing. Normal hours Saturday so hit us up then!

New wines in the door:

Pellegrino Traimari Frizzante: It’s back! Fresh and playful and frizzante, Traimari is chock full of floral and citrus aromatics. White peach, lilac and a hint of pink grapefruit together with lemon-lime acidity make for an incredibly bright bottle. The dry finish puts a bow on top of this delightful bottle. Bonus: this is a perfect pairer for fried foods.

Vera Vino Verde: Imagine yourself perched on one of the many rooftop bars in Lisbon. With a slight fizz and low alcohol, Vera Vinho Verde is clean and lively with notes of grapefruit, lime and lemon.

Tutiac Cote Ocean Rose: Bright, breezy and just a little bit flirty, Cote Ocean Rose brings coastal vibes straight to your glass with juicy bursts of strawberry and a splash of citrus. Effortlessly charming, it's exactly the kind of bottle that insists on sunshine and pairs best with a second bottle.

Limited Addition Pink Crush: When we tasted with Bree Stock, we were immediately taken by her coolness (she pulled off a stocking cap effortlessly) and her down-to-earth nature (she personally foot treads most of her wines!). Bree has lived and made wine in more countries than you can imagine - she's Aussie by birth, but has settled in the Willamette Valley where she works with a dozen vineyards and 20 varieties to showcase cool climate wines. Pink Crush is loveable in a Shirley Temple meets red Jolly Rancher mash-up. Unfiltered/Unfined, Native Yeast, Vegan.

What we’re drinking this week:

Yeah, yeah, it's hot out. I'm already hibernating in my basement in an attempt to not fully blast the air conditioning just yet.. it's gonna get cold again next week right? When the weather is taking me on this roller coaster it makes me want to settle in with a good book, a cat on my lap, some comfort food, and a good glass of wine while I wait for things to just figure themselves out. Yes, I did say comfort food, and if you didn't think of pizza then I don't understand. So we're looking for a good wine to pair with pizza - what better option than Field Recordings Pizza Nights?

Field Recordings is one of many projects from famed CA winemaker Andrew Jones. (Fans of our Fableist Cabernet Sauvignon - that's also his.) Andrew uses his Field Recordings project to source grapes from diamonds-in-the rough vineyards all over the Central Coast and produce small quantities of passion project wine. For Pizza Nights he got together with NJ pizza joint Talula's Pizza to design a wine specifically for pairing with their cuisine. Pizza Nights is 100% Gamay (yes, the grape of Beaujolais) and packed with bright, crunchy red fruit (raspberry, cranberry, cherry), soft tannins, and lively acidity. It does, yes, pair well with pizza. But it also pairs great with inconsistent weather and a low hum of general existential dread. Plus, it's noted specifically as a chillable red, so go ahead and pop it into the fridge about 30 minutes before you're going to open it for a nice, refreshing cool treat.

This week’s writeup is brought to you by Julie.

SHOP OUR ENTIRE WINE SELECTION HERE
 

April Tasting Events are spring ready!

 

Thanks for your business! See you soon, Beth, Julie & Aidan and the entire Brew Crew
